2606.27226.title: Ask, Don't Judge: Binary Questions for Interpretable LLM Evaluation and Self-Improvement
2606.27226.paper_summary: Evaluating LLM outputs remains a major bottleneck in NLP: human evaluation is expensive and slow, lexical metrics correlate poorly with human judgments on open-ended generation, and holistic LLM judges often produce opaque scores that are hard to debug. We propose BINEVAL, a framework that decomposes evaluation criteria into atomic binary questions and aggregates the resulting verdicts into interpretable, multi-dimensional scores. Given a task prompt, a meta-prompt generates fine-grained evaluation questions, and an LLM answers them independently for each output, yielding transparent question-level feedback together with calibrated overall scores. This decomposition makes evaluation easier to inspect, easier to diagnose, and directly usable for prompt improvement. Across SummEval, Topical-Chat, and QAGS, BINEVAL matches or outperforms strong baselines including UniEval and G-Eval, with especially strong results on factual consistency benchmarks such as QAGS. Beyond competitive correlation with human judgments, BINEVAL better matches human score distributions and avoids the ceiling effects common in prior LLM judges, leading to better discrimination between borderline and clearly flawed outputs. We further show that the same question-level feedback supports iterative prompt optimization, improving evaluator prompts on summarization and generation prompts on IFBench under both self-update and cross-model update settings. Overall, BINEVAL provides a task-agnostic, training-free, and interpretable evaluation framework that combines strong empirical performance with practical diagnostic and optimization value.
2606.27226.paper_summary_zh: 評估 LLM 的輸出仍然是 NLP 中的一個主要瓶頸：人類評估成本高且速度慢，詞彙指標與人類對開放式生成的判斷相關性差，而整體 LLM 評估者往往產生難以調試的模糊分數。我們提出了 BINEVAL，一個將評估標準分解為原子二元問題並將結果判決匯總為可解釋的多維分數的框架。給定一個任務提示，元提示生成細緻的評估問題，LLM 獨立地為每個輸出回答這些問題，產生透明的問題級反饋以及經過校準的整體分數。這種分解使得評估更容易檢查、更容易診斷，並且可以直接用於提示改進。在 SummEval、Topical-Chat 和 QAGS 中，BINEVAL 的表現與 UniEval 和 G-Eval 等強基準相匹配或超越，特別是在 QAGS 等事實一致性基準上表現強勁。除了與人類判斷的競爭性相關性外，BINEVAL 更好地匹配人類分數分佈，並避免了先前 LLM 評估者常見的天花板效應，從而在邊界和明顯有缺陷的輸出之間提供了更好的區分。我們進一步展示了相同的問題級反饋支持迭代提示優化，在自我更新和跨模型更新設置下改善了總結和生成提示的評估者提示。總體而言，BINEVAL 提供了一個任務無關、無需訓練且可解釋的評估框架，結合了強大的實證性能與實用的診斷和優化價值。
2606.27226.author: Sangwoo Cho et.al.
2606.27226.authors: Sangwoo Cho, Kushal Chawla, Pengshan Cai, Zefang Liu, Chenyang Zhu, Shi-Xiong Zhang, Sambit Sahu
